title: Client Report - What’s in a name?
text:
Let’s look at how names have changed over time. For instance, the name Travis wasn’t very common until 1910, but by 1990, it became really popular. After that, it dropped off quite a bit. If you meet someone named Brittany, they’re probably around 35, based on what the data tells us. It’s harder to get exact numbers due to data limitations, though.
When we look at Christian names like Mary, Martha, Peter, and Paul, there’s a clear decline starting around 1956. It’s interesting to think about why these names became less popular. And if you’re curious about the influence of movies on names, look at Maverick from ‘Top Gun’. It was almost forgotten, but after the movie came out, it started to get popular again. This data really shows how our culture and trends change over time


Read and format project data
# Include and execute your code here
df = pd.read_csv("https://raw.githubusercontent.com/byuidatascience/data4names/master/data-raw/names_year/names_year.csv")
#show(df)

title: Client Report - What’s in a name?
text:
Let’s look at how names have changed over time. For instance, the name Travis wasn’t very common until 1910, but by 1990, it became really popular. After that, it dropped off quite a bit. If you meet someone named Brittany, they’re probably around 35, based on what the data tells us. It’s harder to get exact numbers due to data limitations, though.
When we look at Christian names like Mary, Martha, Peter, and Paul, there’s a clear decline starting around 1956. It’s interesting to think about why these names became less popular. And if you’re curious about the influence of movies on names, look at Maverick from ‘Top Gun’. It was almost forgotten, but after the movie came out, it started to get popular again. This data really shows how our culture and trends change over time


Read and format project data
# Include and execute your code here
df = pd.read_csv("https://raw.githubusercontent.com/byuidatascience/data4names/master/data-raw/names_year/names_year.csv")
#show(df)

title: Client Report - What’s in a name?
text:
QUESTION|TASK 1
How does your name at your birth year compare to its use historically?
Starting in 1910 till 1962, where children being named Travis began to increase. By the year 1978, 6,849 children had been named Travis. The transition between 1978 and 1979 would then experience a 32% increase in usage. For the years between 1979 and 1990, the name would see alot of fluctuation in how often it was used. 1990 would mark the peak usage as well as see the name’s continual decline till the end of the data set. Specifically, between 1990 and 1998, my birthday, the name saw a 66% decline in usage. 2007 broke the continuous declines, with a small increase before continuing the decreasing decline.


Read and format data
# Include and execute your code here
travis = df[df['name'] == 'Travis'] 
hl = travis[travis['year'] == 1998]
# peak = travis[travis['year'] == 1990]

# total_df1 = travis[travis['year'] == 1990]['Total'].values[0]
# total_df2 = travis[travis['year'] == 1998]['Total'].values[0]
# total_df3 = travis[travis['year'] == 1978]['Total'].values[0]
# total_df4 = travis[travis['year'] == 1979]['Total'].values[0]

# percent_decrease = ((total_df1 - total_df2) / total_df1) * 100
# print(percent_decrease)
# percent_decrease2 = ((total_df4 - total_df3) / total_df1) * 100
# print(percent_decrease2)
# show(travis)




Code
# Include and execute your code here

fig = px.line(
    travis, 
    x = 'year', 
    y = 'Total',
    title = 'Travis Name Usage Trends since 1910',
    markers = False, 
    line_shape = 'spline'
) 
fig.add_scatter(
    x=hl['year'], 
    y=hl['Total'],
    mode='markers', 
    marker=dict(color='darkgoldenrod', size=10),
    showlegend=False
)
fig.add_annotation(
    x=1999, 
    y=max(hl['Total'])+150, 
    text="My Birthday",
    showarrow=True,
    arrowhead=1,
    ax=20,  
    ay=-30,
    arrowcolor="darkgoldenrod",
    font= dict(color="darkgoldenrod")
)
fig.add_vline(
    x=1990, 
    line_width=2, 
    line_dash="dash", 
    line_color="red"
)
fig.add_annotation(
    x=1991, 
    y=max(travis['Total'])+180, 
    text="Peak",
    showarrow=True,
    arrowhead=1,
    ax=20,  
    ay=-30,
    arrowcolor="red",
    font= dict(color="red")
)
fig.update_layout(
    plot_bgcolor = 'white',
    xaxis_title = 'Year',
    yaxis_title = 'Total', 
    title_x = 0.5,
    title_font = dict(size = 24)
)
fig.update_xaxes( 
    showgrid = True, 
    gridcolor = 'lightgray'
) 
fig.update_yaxes(
    showgrid=True,  
    gridcolor='lightgray'  
)

fig.show()



                                                
This graph shows the trends when it came to naming children Travis since 1910.

title: Client Report - What’s in a name?
text:
QUESTION|TASK 2
If you talked to someone named Brittany on the phone, what is your guess of his or her age? What ages would you not guess?
Our analysis has proved, through the chart, that the age of most Brittanys are at this date is 35. Additionally, it would be safe to assume that most Brittanys are also within 30 to 40 years old. On that note, I found it difficult to get a good mean and standard deviation due to the design of the data and my stuggles collecting a mean passed on occurrences.


Read and format data
# Include and execute your code here
brit = df[df['name'] == 'Brittany'] 
c_yr = datetime.now().year

brit['Age'] = c_yr - brit['year']
#show(brit)
peak2 = brit[brit['Age'] == 34]




Code
# Include and execute your code here
fig2 = px.line(
    brit, 
    x = 'Age', 
    y = 'Total',
    title = 'Most Likely Age of a Brittany',
    markers = False, 
    line_shape = 'spline'
)
fig2.add_scatter(
    x=peak2['Age'], 
    y=peak2['Total'],
    mode='markers', 
    marker=dict(color='darkgoldenrod', size=10),
    showlegend=False
)
fig2.add_annotation(
    x=peak2['Age'].iloc[0] + 1, 
    y=peak2['Total'].iloc[0] + 1000, 
    text="Peak",
    showarrow=True,
    arrowhead=1,
    ax=20,  
    ay=-30,
    arrowcolor="darkgoldenrod",
    font= dict(color="darkgoldenrod")
)
fig2.update_layout(
    plot_bgcolor = 'white',
    xaxis_title = 'Age',
    yaxis_title = 'Total', 
    title_x = 0.5,
    title_font = dict(size = 24)
)
fig2.update_xaxes( 
    showgrid = True, 
    gridcolor = 'lightgray'
) 
fig2.update_yaxes(
    showgrid=True,  
    gridcolor='lightgray'  
)

fig2.show()



                                                
This Chart demonstrates the ages of Brittanys in the United States

title: Client Report - What’s in a name?
text:
QUESTION|TASK 3
Mary, Martha, Peter, and Paul are all Christian names. From 1920 - 2000, compare the name usage of each of the four names. What trends do you notice?
In the chart below, we have the opportunity to see the peak trends of all the some of the major christian names. More interesting, is that by 1956, all the Christian names start to see trend that continue to decrease accross the board. This poses the question of what was occurring in the United States culture that has led to varing type of decreases. Also interesting is that Paul saw an increase starting in 1936 until its peak. The greates change in trends would be Mary, who saw a decrease of 47,607 from 1950 to 1999. As a result I conclude that christian names are on a decreasing trend.


Read and format data
# Include and execute your code here
chris = df[
    (df['name'] == 'Mary') |
    (df['name'] == 'Martha') |
    (df['name'] == 'Peter') |
    (df['name'] == 'Paul')
] 
chris = chris[
    (chris['year'] >= 1920) & 
    (chris['year'] <= 2000)
]
mpeak = chris[
    (chris['year'] == 1950) &
    (chris['name'] == 'Mary')
]
ppeak = chris[
    (chris['year'] == 1954) &
    (chris['name'] == 'Paul')
]
peakm = chris[
    (chris['year'] == 1947) &
    (chris['name'] == 'Martha')
]
peakp = chris[
    (chris['year'] == 1956) &
    (chris['name'] == 'Peter')
]
#show(chris)




code
fig3 = px.line(
    chris, 
    x = 'year', 
    y = 'Total',
    color='name',
    title = 'Christian Names Trends from 1920 - 2000',
    markers = False, 
    line_shape = 'spline'
)
fig3.add_scatter(
    x=mpeak['year'], 
    y=mpeak['Total'],
    mode='markers', 
    marker=dict(color='darkgoldenrod', size=10),
    showlegend=False
)
fig3.add_scatter(
    x=ppeak['year'], 
    y=ppeak['Total'],
    mode='markers', 
    marker=dict(color='darkgoldenrod', size=10),
    showlegend=False
)
fig3.add_scatter(
    x=peakp['year'], 
    y=peakp['Total'],
    mode='markers', 
    marker=dict(color='darkgoldenrod', size=10),
    showlegend=False
)
fig3.add_scatter(
    x=peakm['year'], 
    y=peakm['Total'],
    mode='markers', 
    marker=dict(color='darkgoldenrod', size=10),
    showlegend=False
)
fig3.add_annotation(
    x=1953, 
    y=55000,
    text="Peaks",
    showarrow=False,
    xshift=10,
    font= dict(color="darkgoldenrod")  
)
fig3.update_layout(
    plot_bgcolor = 'white',
    xaxis_title = 'Year',
    yaxis_title = 'Total', 
    title_x = 0.5,
    title_font = dict(size = 24),
    legend_title_text='Names'
)
fig3.update_xaxes( 
    showgrid = True, 
    gridcolor = 'lightgray'
) 
fig3.update_yaxes(
    showgrid=True,  
    gridcolor='lightgray'  
)

fig3.show()



                                                
This Chart to demonstrates the use of Christian names from 1900 to 2000 in the United States

title: Client Report - What’s in a name?
text:
QUESTION|TASK 4
Think of a unique name from a famous movie. Plot the usage of that name and see how changes line up with the movie release. Does it look like the movie had an effect on usage?
For a famous movie, I chose to do Maverick from Top Gun. As a result, I got to see an interesting trend. At the start of the data, Maverick was a rarely used name, infact, from 1961 to 1986, there were no new names of Maverick. That is when the first Top Gun movie is released. The movie resurrects the name and from there that name increases to over 2000 until the end of the dataset.


Read and format data
# Include and execute your code here
Maverick = df[
    (df['name'] == 'Maverick') &
    (df['year'] > 1950)
]
# show(Maverick)




code
# Include and execute your code here
fig3 = px.line(
    Maverick, 
    x = 'year', 
    y = 'Total',
    title = 'Top Gun Name Maverick',
    markers = False, 
    line_shape = 'spline'
)
fig3.add_annotation(
    x=1987, 
    y= 1100, 
    text="1st Movie Release",
    showarrow=True,
    arrowhead=1,
    ax=80,  
    ay=-30,
    arrowcolor="red",
    font= dict(color="red")
)
fig3.add_vline(
    x=1986, 
    line_width=2, 
    line_dash="dash", 
    line_color="red"
)
fig3.add_scatter(
    x=[1961], 
    y=[1],
    mode='markers', 
    marker=dict(color='darkgoldenrod', size=10),
    showlegend=False
)
fig3.add_scatter(
    x=[1986], 
    y=[1],
    mode='markers', 
    marker=dict(color='darkgoldenrod', size=10),
    showlegend=False
)
fig3.add_annotation(
    x=1970, 
    y= 2, 
    text="0 Named Maverick",
    showarrow=True,
    arrowhead=1,
    ax=20,  
    ay=-30,
    arrowcolor="darkgoldenrod",
    font= dict(color="darkgoldenrod")
)
fig3.update_layout(
    plot_bgcolor = 'white',
    xaxis_title = 'Year',
    yaxis_title = 'Total', 
    title_x = 0.5,
    title_font = dict(size = 24),
    legend_title_text='Names'
)
fig3.update_xaxes( 
    showgrid = True, 
    gridcolor = 'lightgray'
) 
fig3.update_yaxes(
    showgrid=True,  
    gridcolor='lightgray'  
)

fig3.show()



                                                
This chart shows the trends of the data of the name of Maverick in relation to the first Top Gun movie

text:

2019 - Present




Amazon DSP Delivery Driver | Metropolitan logistics | Sterling, VA August 2022 - December 2022 * Developed good customer service skills through delivering up to 600 packages to customer instructions * Displayed an ability to work and efficiently manage time for up to 10 hours * Enhanced physical fitness through ability to lift packages weighing up to 50 lbs all day * Refined attention to detail by demonstrating highest level of integrity, compliance, and adherence within delivery/transportation services * Honed strong verbal communication skills while working with a team of 15
Delivery Technician | Mountain Hot Tub | Helena, MT September 2021 - December 2021 * Delivered and installed up to 3 hot tubs by working in teams including filling and connecting * Troubleshot onsite problems, offering immediate and long-term solutions
Customer Care and Loyalty Agent | SafeStreetsUSA | Rexburg, ID March 2021 - June 2021 * Resolved calls from national customers while balancing company policies
Service Volunteer | Church of Jesus Christ of Latter Day Saints | Tijuana, Baja California March 2021 - June 2021 August 2017 - August 2019
